headline: Pope's first day in Algeria marred by failed suicide attack near Algiers
description: Two men blew themselves up on Monday in downtown Blida, about 40 kilometers south of the Algerian capital, without causing any casualties other than themselves. Leo XIV had arrived just hours earlier in Algiers for the first visit by a pope to the country.

headline: American Catholics outraged at Trump's attack of pope and AI-generated image of himself as Christ
Hungary enters new era following Orban's defeat
Low turnout could shape the 2027 French presidential race
description: Abstainers from the 2026 municipal elections could fuel a protest vote in the presidential election, writes Anne Muxel, emeritus research professor at CEVIPOF, based on the French electoral survey by Ipsos BVA-CESI Ecole d'Ingénieurs for Le Monde, CEVIPOF and the Jean Jaurès Foundation.

headline: Strait of Hormuz blockade poses threat to food security
description: The war in the Middle East has led to a domino effect, threatening to worsen global hunger. Geopolitical tensions are exposing the strong dependence of food systems on countries that produce fossil fuels and synthetic fertilizers.

headline: France and UK to co-host Strait of Hormuz talks Friday
description: President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will co-chair the meeting to discuss a plan to 'restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz when security conditions allow.'

headline: Jordan Bardella, a princess and 'Paris Match': The story of a PR gamble
description: The relationship between Marine Le Pen's lieutenant and an aristocratic student raised in the international jet set made the front page of 'Paris Match.' The media empire of Vincent Bolloré has seized the opportunity to 'glamorize' the likely far-right presidential candidate.

Italian PM announces suspension of defense agreement with Israel
Will California's next governor be a Republican?
description: The crowded field of Democratic candidates running in the gubernatorial election raises the possibility that a Trump-aligned Republican could win in November in this deeply progressive state.

headline: The planning battle to revive Tour Montparnasse, Paris's only skyscraper
description: After more than 10 years of negotiations between Paris City Hall and the tower's co-owners, the transformation of the now-empty Tour Montparnasse and its property complex is set to begin in autumn 2026.
byline: Audrey Lagadec,  Emeline Cazi and  Isabelle Regnier

headline: Parenting: Why the morning rush drives us crazy
description: Le Monde's parenting columnist, Clara Georges, breaks down one of her typically hectic family mornings, drawing on the insights of philosopher Pierre Vesperini and the solutions proposed by feminist author Titiou Lecoq.

headline: 'Lebanon's resilience is celebrated, as if survival were admirable rather than imposed'

In Hungary, a welcome defeat for illiberalism
